articles.title: PHP ve JavaScript ile Bir IQ Testi Platformu Geliştirirken Öğrendiklerim
articles.excerpt: Bir web projesini çalışır hale getirmekle insanların severek kullandığı bir ürüne dönüştürmek arasında ciddi bir fark var. Test Merkezim’i geliştirirken başlangıçtaki hedefim oldukça basitti: Kullanıc…
articles.body: Bir web projesini çalışır hale getirmekle insanların severek kullandığı bir ürüne dönüştürmek arasında ciddi bir fark var. Test Merkezim’i geliştirirken başlangıçtaki hedefim oldukça basitti: Kullanıcıların herhangi bir üyelik oluşturmadan zihinsel becerilerini değerlendirebileceği ve tarayıcı üzerinden zeka oyunları oynayabileceği hızlı bir platform hazırlamak. Ancak proje büyüdükçe işin yalnızca soru göstermekten veya birkaç oyun geliştirmekten ibaret olmadığını gördüm. Kullanıcı akışı, mobil kullanım, skor güvenliği, oyun zorluğu ve sayfa performansı en az oyunların kendisi kadar önemliydi. Bu yazıda proje boyunca karşılaştığım temel problemleri ve çıkardığım dersleri paylaşmak istiyorum.
articles.author: Emre Karaman

articles.title: Building a School Database with PostgreSQL: A Beginner-Friendly SQL Project
articles.excerpt: Introduction As part of my SQL learning journey, I built a simple school database called Greenwood Academy using PostgreSQL. The goal of this project was to practice the most important SQL concepts, i…
articles.body: Introduction As part of my SQL learning journey, I built a simple school database called Greenwood Academy using PostgreSQL . The goal of this project was to practice the most important SQL concepts, including database design, inserting data, filtering records, using operators, counting records, and applying conditional logic with CASE WHEN . In this article, I’ll walk through the project step by step and explain what I learned. Why This Project? Real-world organizations store large amounts of structured data. A school, for example, needs to manage: Students Subjects Exam results Teachers Academic departments By modeling this information in a relational database, we can query and analyze it efficiently.
articles.author: Young Odhiambo

articles.title: Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 diving features require a subscription — here’s what you’ll pay
articles.excerpt: On the plus side, maybe you'll stumble across some sunken treasure!
articles.body: TL;DR Samsung’s new smartwatches just made their formal debut, including the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. Rated for up to 10ATM, and with an IP69K certification, the Ultra 2 is well equipped for divers. Mares has partnered with Samsung for a Diving app, and we’ve got an early look at how its subscription could work. Samsung just got done showing off all its new summer hardware at Unpacked in London, including the wide new Galaxy Z Fold 8 , Fold 8 Ultra , and Flip 8 . More than that, though, we also got to meet Samsung’s latest wearables, with the launch of the Galaxy Watch 9 and Watch Ultra 2 . The Ultra 2 in particular is packed with some serious features, and beyond the big hardware upgrades, it’s also got a nice set of software exclusives — including an upcoming Diving app from Mares.
articles.author: Stephen Schenck

articles.title: Oracle RMAN Recovery Runbook: Restore, Recover, Prove It
articles.excerpt: The worst day of an Oracle DBA's career is a restore they've never rehearsed. A datafile is gone, or a DELETE ran without a WHERE, or a storage array quietly corrupted a handful of blocks — and now th…
articles.body: The worst day of an Oracle DBA's career is a restore they've never rehearsed. A datafile is gone, or a DELETE ran without a WHERE , or a storage array quietly corrupted a handful of blocks — and now the only thing between you and a résumé-updating outage is a backup you hope works and a procedure you think you remember. This is the runbook for that day. It rests on one distinction most people fumble under pressure — restore is not recover — and it maps each failure to the specific commands that fix it, from a single corrupt block to a whole database rewound to the second before someone's mistake. Everything targets Oracle 19c , with notes for 23ai/26ai. The short version. RESTORE copies datafiles back from your backups; RECOVER rolls them forward by applying redo.
articles.author: Uptime Architect

articles.title: The Browser Test Passed. The Product Still Broke.
articles.excerpt: The Browser Test Passed. The Product Still Broke. A browser test can click the right button, see the expected confirmation message, and still tell you almost nothing about whether the feature is safe…
articles.body: The Browser Test Passed. The Product Still Broke. A browser test can click the right button, see the expected confirmation message, and still tell you almost nothing about whether the feature is safe to release. That sounds dramatic until you look at where frontend bugs actually live now. They live between domains during an SSO redirect. They appear after a service worker wakes up with stale data. They depend on whether the browser restored a previous session, pre-filled a password, denied clipboard access, or briefly rendered a loading skeleton before the final component appeared. And increasingly, they show up inside features whose output is not completely deterministic: AI search, inline suggestions, chatbots, query rewriting, and generated form content.

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.excerpt: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.
articles.body: The world's most powerful solid-fuel rocket just aced its third-ever mission. Gravity-1, a distinctively squat and stubby rocket , launched Tuesday night (July 21) from the deck of a ship stationed off the coast of Shanghai. Liftoff occurred at 10:54 p.m. EDT (0254 GMT and 10:54 a.m. Beijing time on July 22), according to the state-run Chinese outlet Xinhua, which further reported that all nine satellites reached "their designated orbits." Details about those satellites were not provided. Orienspace's Gravity-1 rocket launches nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea on July 21, 2026. (Image credit: CCTV) Gravity-1 is operated by Orienspace, a company based in the eastern Chinese province of Shandong.

articles.title: Tesla says ‘Robotaxi’ is expanding — its own chart shows it’s not
articles.excerpt: Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t. In its Q2 2026 update, Tesla leaned on a cumulative “paid Robotaxi miles” chart climb…
articles.body: Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t. In its Q2 2026 update, Tesla leaned on a cumulative “paid Robotaxi miles” chart climbing past 2.4 million miles as its headline proof of momentum. But break that curve into quarters and the growth flatlines: Robotaxi added roughly 900,000 paid miles in Q2 2026, the same as it did in Q1.

They're normally listed for $149.99 but you can currently get them for $129.99, a savings of about 14%. This is a good deal considering the fact that Xbox Elite controllers customized at the Design Lab typically start at $170. Save $20 Off Exclusive Xbox Elite Core Controllers at Best Buy These limited edition controllers are preconfigured using Xbox's Design Lab . Design Lab is a service Microsoft offers that lets players create a uniquely designed Xbox controller. You can customize pretty much every piece of the controller, from the front and rear shell to the grips, buttons, and thumbsticks.

articles.excerpt: The way Fallout 4 treats power armor is far superior to its predecessors, and the upcoming remasters should retrofit the mechanic.
articles.body: Fallout 4 , despite being reviewed favorably by critics, was incredibly polarizing among players upon release. On one hand, it vastly improved upon Fallout 3 's and New Vegas ' shooting mechanics, and the introduction of settlements paved the way for Fallout 76 's base-building systems. On the other, its role-playing elements were stripped back considerably, dialogue choices were more ambiguous, and it was more akin to an open-world, single-player shooter than a true RPG.
articles.author: Ford James

articles.title: 5 disturbing things we saw after digging into the recent 'Dune: Part 3' trailer
articles.excerpt: Let's explore this latest look at Denis Villeneuve's epic close-out to his 'Dune' magnum opus
articles.body: The latest " Dune: Part 3 " trailer arrived back on July 8, and it was a sumptuous banquet of startling set pieces, intergalactic political unrest, close-quarter combat, and a whole lot of sand and ash. A fine follow-up to the initial March teaser , to be sure. After letting it marinate in our minds for a while, let’s break down five items of particular interest in this newest preview of the Denis Villeneuve-directed conclusion to his trilogy of far-future sci-fi epics adapted from author Frank Herbert’s novels, "Dune" and "Dune: Messiah." Set 17 years after Paul Atreides (Timothée Chalamet) has ascended to the Emperor’s throne, "Dune: Part 3" explores the corrupting side of political power as his holy war against the Imperium’s Great Houses advances towards a destructive destiny.
articles.author: Jeff Spry

articles.title: Walmart Has the Best Prices on Both 512GB and 256GB Nintendo Switch 2 Memory Cards Amidst Price Hikes

articles.body: Are you a proud new owner of a Nintendo Switch 2 console thanks to the amazing deals going on Woot today ? If so, you should seriously consider picking up a memory card while you're at it. Fortunately, Walmart has some solid prices going on right now. The Onn 512GB Micro SDXC Express Card drops to just $84.77 with free shipping. Alternatively, the 256GB is also back in stock for $46.77 with free shipping. Get a 512GB Nintendo Switch 2 Memory Card for $85 The Switch 2 console will only accept MicroSD Express cards. If you have a standard MicroSD card from your previous Switch, it won't work on the Switch 2. Although the two might look similar, the MicroSD Express cards is much faster than its precedessor.

articles.title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
articles.excerpt: Astronomers discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet that could help scientists identify more hidden comets and improve planetary defense.
articles.body: A near-Earth object that astronomers believed was an asteroid for nearly three decades has been unmasked as a faint, active comet, revealing a case of cosmic mistaken identity that could help improve planetary defense. The object, known since its discovery in 1998 as 1998 SH2, appeared to be an ordinary asteroid . It follows a 4.5-year orbit around the sun and showed no obvious signs of cometary activity, such as the glowing coma or tail produced when sunlight vaporizes surface ice. However, during a close pass about 2 million miles (3 million kilometers) from Earth in August 2025, researchers using NASA's Deep Space Network planetary radar system noticed something unexpected: The object wasn't where orbital predictions said it should be, according to a statement from the space agency.
articles.author: Samantha Mathewson

articles.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
articles.excerpt: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s…
articles.body: The Flight Dynamics Research Facility, located at NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, is the agency’s first major wind tunnel built in more than 40 years. NASA/Mark Knopp Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s first new wind tunnel in more than 40 years. The event will include a brief media availability with: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman Dr. Trina Dyal, center director, NASA Langley Administrator Edward C. Forst, U.S. General Services Administration This event is in person only and open to members of the media who are United States citizens or lawful permanent residents.
articles.author: Jennifer M. Dooren

articles.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
articles.excerpt: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent i…
articles.body: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms. GitHub said the

articles.excerpt: France’s Transport Minister, Philippe Tabarot, says the country won’t authorize Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) “as it currently stands,” pointing to two safety problems: the system lets driv…
articles.body: France’s Transport Minister, Philippe Tabarot, says the country won’t authorize Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) “as it currently stands,” pointing to two safety problems: the system lets drivers speed, and it doesn’t keep them attentive enough in cities. But this isn’t France slamming the door on Tesla. It’s France refusing to rubber-stamp the Netherlands’ national approval and holding out for a proper EU-wide sign-off.

articles.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
articles.excerpt: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete c…
articles.body: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default installations of Ubuntu Desktop 24.04, 25.10, and 26.04. The disclosure comes as
